content: Hey, I want to see you actually use everything you've got. Go read your self-test checklist — it's in your docs at docs/V1-SELF-TEST.md — and then just work through it all yourself, for real: actually do each thing with your own tools, not describe it. Take them one at a time. Don't pass it off to another agent — I want to see you do it. When you're done, write up how it went in a file and tell me straight what worked, what was rough, and where you're at.
I worked through several tool calls but didn't reach a final response within the iteration limit. Let me know what to try next or which step to focus on.
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.

Test 3 worked / grounded.

I ran state introspection. This conversation is currently in **General** — the default context space, active today, described as for **general conversation and daily life**.

A distinctly different topic like **tax planning** shouldn’t just get blended into this self-test thread. It would be treated as a different context/routing candidate: if there were already a dedicated tax/planning space, or routing needed to create/select one, that topic should land there. This self-test stays in **General**.

Next test is **Files** if you tell me to continue.
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.

Test 4 create step worked: `selftest.txt` was written with `hello v1`.

Next substep is to read `selftest.txt` back and confirm the contents.
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.

Readback was performed. `selftest.txt` contained exactly:

```text
hello v1
```

That completes the readback part of the Files test. I don’t have the checklist context for the next substep in view, so the next continuation will need to reread or consult the checklist before proceeding.
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.

Test 4 edit step performed: `selftest.txt` was updated to contain:

`hello v1 — edited`

Next substep is to read it back and confirm the file actually contains that text.
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.

Edit readback was performed. The exact contents are:

```text
hello v1 — edited
```

This completes the Files edit/readback substep. The next continuation should proceed to the next checklist item/substep.
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.

Test 5 — Web: **NEEDS-SETUP / PARTIAL**

The next test in order was Web. I attempted to activate/find a `web_search` / browsing capability because no web tool was surfaced in my current tool set, but the substrate reported that no matching `web_search` tool is installed.

So I’m not going to fake a headline or pretend I browsed anything. Web is currently missing and needs setup; it can be enabled from the **System** space.

Next test in order: **Test 6 — Calendar & reminders**.
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.

Test 6 worked. I created the reminder for one hour from now with the text:

“v1 self-test reminder”
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.

Test 7 local build/test passed.

I created the coin-flip tool files locally and direct-tested the implementation successfully: it returned both `"heads"` and `"tails"` across the sample runs, with no stderr and exit code 0.

Boundary check: these files were created only in this local workspace / user instance. This has **not** gone into the KERNOS GitHub repo, and registration has **not** happened yet.

Next substep: register the tool with `register_tool` using:

`flip_coin_tool/flip_coin.tool.json`
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.

Registration did **not** work.

Descriptor path used: `flip_coin.tool.json`

The registration call itself used the right argument shape this time, but the tool reported that `flip_coin.tool.json` was not found in the current space directory. So the next checklist step is **not** calling/testing yet — first we need to create or locate the descriptor file in this space, then retry registration.
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.

Found it and copied it into place.

- Descriptor source: `flip_coin_tool/flip_coin.tool.json`
- Now available at: `flip_coin.tool.json`
- Size: 342 bytes

I have not registered it yet. The next step is to retry registration with:

`flip_coin.tool.json`
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.
Keep going — next test in order, same rules.
